name: Fort Stage
description: The largest stage, on the open waterfront field outside the fort walls. Headliner sets and the biggest crowds land here, with wide-open sightlines to Narragansett Bay and very little natural shade.

seating.summary: General admission standing and blanket field; no supplied seating outside the accessibility area.

shade.summary: Largely open and exposed; bring sun protection. Shade is limited to the field edges.

blankets.summary: Festival-wide 6×8-foot blanket maximum applies; stage-specific no-blanket zones still need verification.

chairs.summary: Festival-wide chair rules apply; no-chair-zone boundaries still need verification.

surface.summary: Grass field that can be uneven and soft after rain.

accessibility.summary: Reserved accessibility area beside the sound board; seating is not supplied, and each patron may be accompanied by one guest because space is limited.

amenities: Nearest to the main food and vendor row

name: Harbor Stage
description: A mid-size stage set right on the water, known for intimate sets, collaborations, and surprise guests. Sightlines are good but capacity fills quickly for popular acts.

seating.summary: General admission standing and blankets; no supplied seating outside the accessibility area.

shade.summary: Mostly open with some shade along the sides; the audience area is largely exposed.

blankets.summary: Festival-wide 6×8-foot blanket maximum applies; practical placement still needs verification.

chairs.summary: Festival-wide chair rules apply; local no-chair zones still need verification.

surface.summary: Grass and packed ground near the waterfront.

accessibility.summary: Accessibility-specific chairs are available to wristbanded patrons, but seating availability is not guaranteed and chairs should not be saved for absent guests.

name: Quad Stage
description: Set inside the fort walls, the Quad is a compact, high-energy stage with a boxed-in courtyard feel. Sound carries well but capacity is limited, so plan to arrive early for buzzy acts.

seating.summary: General admission standing and blankets; no supplied seating outside the accessibility area.

shade.summary: The fort walls provide some shade at the edges; the center is largely open.

blankets.summary: Festival-wide 6×8-foot blanket maximum applies; practical placement still needs verification.

chairs.summary: Festival-wide chair rules apply; local no-chair zones still need verification.

surface.summary: Grass courtyard inside the fort; can bottleneck at the entrances.

accessibility.summary: Accessibility-specific chairs are available to wristbanded patrons, but seating availability is not guaranteed and chairs should not be saved for absent guests.

name: Foundation Stage
description: A small stage inside the fort dedicated to the Newport Festivals Foundation's music-education programming, emerging artists, and short talks. Sets are brief and it is one of the easiest places to catch a rising name up close.

seating.summary: Small, intimate footprint; standing and limited informal seating. Verify before launch.

shade.summary: Partly sheltered by the surrounding fort structure. Verify before launch.

blankets.summary: Festival-wide 6×8-foot blanket maximum applies; space here is tight.

chairs.summary: Festival-wide chair rules apply; the compact footprint limits chair space.

surface.summary: Unknown — verify grass, paved, or mixed surface.

accessibility.summary: Accessibility-specific chairs are available to wristbanded patrons, but seating availability is not guaranteed and chairs should not be saved for absent guests.

name: Bike Stage
description: The festival's smallest, most casual stage near the entrance and bike valet. It opens the day with an open-mic slot and hosts short acoustic sets from up-and-coming artists — a low-key spot to discover someone new.

seating.summary: Casual standing; no supplied seating. Verify before launch.

shade.summary: Largely open near the entrance. Verify before launch.

blankets.summary: Festival-wide 6×8-foot blanket maximum applies; this is a stand-and-watch spot.

chairs.summary: Festival-wide chair rules apply; the footprint is small.

surface.summary: Unknown — verify grass or paved surface near the entrance.

accessibility.summary: Accessibility-specific chairs are available to wristbanded patrons, but seating availability is not guaranteed and chairs should not be saved for absent guests.

amenities: Near the entrance and bike valet
